title: sisymbrium pinnatifidum subsp. boryi
intro: Sisymbrium pinnatifidum subsp. boryi, also known as Bory's hedge mustard, is a plant species in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its pinnate leaves and tall, branching stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Sisymbrium pinnatifidum subsp. boryi include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve congestion, and soothe respiratory tract irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make infusions, teas, or decoctions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
